_meta.description: Three standardised anime shot archetypes. Every provider generates these same three 10-second clips for apples-to-apples comparison.

shots.name: Establishing Shot — Silent Wide Scene
shots.prompt: Neon-lit Tokyo rooftop at dusk, distant city hum, a lone shadow walks across the frame. 2D anime style, soft lighting.
shots.notes: Low complexity, ambient motion only. Tests baseline video generation quality and cost.

shots.name: Dialogue Close-Up — Character with Audible Line
shots.prompt: Teenage girl with black hair and a school uniform. She speaks the line: "I finally understand why you came back." Soft lighting, 2D anime style, tight close-up on face.

dialogue.text: I finally understand why you came back.

shots.notes: The critical test. Requires reference image + dialogue audio. Tests lipsync, character fidelity, and voice integration.

shots.name: Action / Multi-Character — Dynamic Two-Character Scene
shots.prompt: Samurai leaps from a moving train, sword drawn, his opponent blocks with a glowing staff. Rain, speed lines, 2D anime style.
shots.notes: High complexity, fast motion, two characters. Tests motion handling and multi-character scenes.
